goal: Get The Idea Guy products live in front of real users — ship Rooms and the website publicly and earn trust as the privacy-first, local-first group app

items.title: Ship the website
items.description: Deploy the marketing site to production. Static export already builds and a Cloudflare Pages playbook exists, so this is mostly execution.
items.impact_rationale: Nothing is live yet; the brand site is the cheapest path to any public presence and the landing point for everything else.
items.effort_rationale: Build pipeline and deploy playbook already exist; ~1 week of execution and polish.

items.title: Onboarding & PWA install polish in Rooms
items.description: Install prompt, first-run hints, and empty states so a first-time visitor understands Rooms without explanation.
items.impact_rationale: Every new user hits this surface first; bad first-run kills the launch regardless of features.

items.title: Ship Rooms to production
items.description: PWA on a real domain plus the relay in production (compaction on, backups), with invite links that work outside localhost.
items.impact_rationale: This is the launch itself — the goal cannot be met without it.
items.effort_rationale: Domain, TLS, relay hosting, backups, and real-world invite testing; ~2 weeks.

items.title: Publish the threat model doc
items.description: Formal adversary profiles (relay operator, network observer, ex-member, device thief) plus honest limitations as a public HTML page — the main trust asset for the privacy-focused launch audience.
items.impact_rationale: Directly supports the trust half of the goal for the privacy-conscious audience the launch targets.
items.effort_rationale: A draft already exists at docs_and_changelog/threat-model.html; remaining work is review, commit, and publishing.

notes.note: Draft exists as untracked docs_and_changelog/threat-model.html; needs review, commit, and a public link once the site ships.
Committed in bf0c473 with biometric unlock coverage added. Remaining: publish publicly when the website ships (RM-001).

items.title: Roommate Ledger template
items.description: Household bills template — the most-voted seeded idea; reuses Trip Split math.
items.impact_rationale: Most-requested template; broadens the launch story but is not a launch gate.
items.effort_rationale: Reuses Trip Split's split math and schema patterns; ~2 weeks.

items.title: Public showcase Backlog room
items.description: Read-only room code embedded on the website — a public roadmap people can vote on, doubling as a live demo of Rooms.
items.impact_rationale: Nice growth/demo asset post-launch, but only matters once the site and Rooms are live.
items.effort_rationale: Mostly wiring an existing room into the website; ~1 week.

items.title: How-to guides section on the website
items.description: Teaching content on the website (roadmap item #4 in IDEA_LOG_AND_TOOLS).
items.impact_rationale: Supports discovery and SEO after launch, but moves nothing until the site is live.
items.effort_rationale: Content writing on an existing site; ~1 week.

items.title: Ship Second Brain
items.description: Deploy as a personal instance plus a public demo vault (relay :4501 hosted).
items.impact_rationale: A second product live helps the portfolio story, but Rooms and the website are the launch focus.
items.effort_rationale: Deployment plus a hosted relay and demo vault; ~2 weeks.

items.title: Push notifications for room changes
items.description: Replace pull-only 'Updated' badges with real push (service worker + push relay endpoint).
items.impact_rationale: Big retention lever for live rooms, but post-launch and technically uncertain.
items.effort_rationale: Service worker push, a new relay endpoint, and platform quirks; ~3 weeks with low confidence.

items.title: Potluck template
items.description: Who brings what, dietary tags, headcount (seeded idea).
items.impact_rationale: Adds template variety post-launch; small audience.
items.effort_rationale: New template on existing schema machinery; ~1.5 weeks.

items.title: Watch Club template
items.description: Book Club for TV: series, episodes, queue (seeded idea).
items.impact_rationale: Adds template variety post-launch; small audience.
items.effort_rationale: New template reusing Book Club patterns; ~2 weeks.

items.title: Idea Validator demo widget
items.description: Interactive widget on the website (roadmap item #5 in IDEA_LOG_AND_TOOLS).
items.impact_rationale: Speculative engagement feature with the lowest confidence in the backlog.
items.effort_rationale: New interactive widget from scratch; ~2 weeks.

items.title: Biometric vault unlock (WebAuthn/PassKey)
items.description: Replace the device PIN with FaceID/TouchID/Windows Hello via platform authenticators; same Argon2id key derivation underneath. Biggest perceived-security win for the least friction.
items.impact_rationale: Banking-app-grade unlock is the strongest perceived-trust signal for the privacy positioning, and better UX than the PIN.
items.effort_rationale: WebAuthn is well supported and the key derivation stays as-is; estimated 3-4 days plus cross-device re-enrollment handling.

notes.note: Gil prioritized this as the first security build — perceived security ('feels super secure') is the centerpiece of his marketing message. Impact bumped 4→5.

Implemented in commit def9122 (WebAuthn PRF wraps the PIN-derived vault key; PIN stays as fallback). Passed 15/15 Playwright checks with a virtual authenticator. Remaining: verify on a real iPhone (Face ID) and Mac (Touch ID) once deployed over HTTPS — WebAuthn needs a secure context, so full validation lands with RM-003.

items.title: One-tap member revocation with silent key migration
items.description: Removing a member rotates the room code and delivers it to remaining members via their pairwise ECDH inboxes; the removed member's code silently stops syncing. Closes the 'removal is cosmetic' gap.
items.impact_rationale: The gap users will actually feel, and the headline differentiator ('removed means actually gone') no competing group app has.
items.effort_rationale: Key rotation plus delivery through existing ECDH inboxes and an offline-member catch-up window; 1-2 weeks.

items.title: Sealed invite links (recipient-bound, one-time)
items.description: Invites to known contacts encrypt the room code to their X25519 public key so a forwarded link is useless; casual link invites fall back to reusable codes with an expiry option (24h/7d).
items.impact_rationale: Closes the forwarded-link anxiety and reads as premium, but matters mainly once invites flow between real users.
items.effort_rationale: Sealed-box encryption over existing contact keys plus an expiry path for open links; ~1 week.

items.title: Per-member Ed25519 content signatures
items.description: Each member slot gets an Ed25519 keypair; every CRDT write is signed and silently verified before applying, making authorship cryptographically provable instead of a claimed field.
items.impact_rationale: Invisible trust infrastructure required before claiming verifiable authorship; prerequisite for marketing the integrity story.
items.effort_rationale: Signing/verification hooks in room-kit sync with ~64 bytes per operation overhead; ~1 week.

items.title: Ratcheting epoch keys (forward secrecy)
items.description: Rooms silently derive a new epoch key every 7 days (or N sync rounds) and delete old keys after a grace period, so a leaked room code only exposes data from the current epoch forward.
items.impact_rationale: Strongest honest privacy claim ('past data stays private even if your code leaks') but invisible to users and post-launch.
items.effort_rationale: Hardest of the security set — key scheduling, grace periods, and re-seeding long-offline devices; 2-3 weeks.
